title: Phtheirospermum glandulosum
intro: Phtheirospermum glandulosum is a plant species native to China, commonly known as the "Chinese snake root" or " Chinese wolf's bane", which is traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, as well as its ability to alleviate fever, headache, and snake bites. The bioactive constituents of Phtheirospermum glandulosum include phenolic compounds, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phtheirospermum glandulosum is often processed into herbal decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites.
taxonomy: Phtheirospermum glandulosum is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This family includes plants such as forget-me-nots and borage. Boraginaceae is a large family of flowering plants, comprising around 2,000 species. These plants are commonly found in temperate and subtropical regions, often in dry and rocky areas. They are known for their small, delicate flowers and often have medicinal properties.
